# Execute and Compare 
set.seed(1983)
comparison_ar_final <- do.call(rbind, lapply(unique(mypts$Crop), evaluate_ar_models))
## AR Evaluation for: 1 
## 0.01073357 0.01 
## 0.009319475 0.01 
## -0.04659281 0.01 
## AR Evaluation for: 2 
## 0.02840259 0.01 
## -0.0009262316 0.01 
## -0.06124316 0.01 
## AR Evaluation for: 3 
## -0.005777284 0.01 
## -0.03494154 0.01 
## AR Evaluation for: 4 
## 0.05186184 0.01 
## -0.01945675 0.01 
## -0.08264479 0.01 
## AR Evaluation for: 5 
## 0.004273939 0.01 
## -0.01208844 0.01 
## AR Evaluation for: 6 
## 0.05529602 0.01 
## 0.02017113 0.01 
## 0.02905516 0.01 
## -0.1220922 0.01 
## AR Evaluation for: 7 
## 0.03832508 0.01 
## 0.009833064 0.01 
## -0.05692883 0.01 
## AR Evaluation for: 8 
## 0.01689216 0.01 
## 0.0001480481 0.01 
## -0.04499869 0.01
print(comparison_ar_final)
##       Crop RMSE_Pooled_AR R2_Pooled_AR RMSE_Spec_AR R2_Spec_AR
## 1    Maize         124.17         0.57       135.70       0.58
## 2     Rice         202.56         0.59       213.72       0.58
## 3  Sorghum         187.00         0.82       241.98       0.71
## 4 B.Millet         173.11         0.93       193.00       0.91
## 5 F.Millet         367.62         0.70       523.83       0.56
## 6    Wheat         284.04         0.73       294.00       0.70
## 7    Beans         208.49         0.75       221.75       0.74
## 8   Potato         132.85         0.70       133.20       0.69
comparison_df_wide_ar <- comparison_ar_final %>%
  rename(
    RMSE_Pooled = RMSE_Pooled_AR,
    R_squared_Pooled = R2_Pooled_AR,
    RMSE_Crop_Specific = RMSE_Spec_AR,
    R_squared_Crop_Specific = R2_Spec_AR)
knitr::kable(comparison_df_wide_ar, digits = 2, caption = "Table 2: Autoregressive (AR) Model Comparison - Pooled vs. Crop-Specific")
Table 2: Autoregressive (AR) Model Comparison - Pooled vs. Crop-Specific
Crop RMSE_Pooled R_squared_Pooled RMSE_Crop_Specific R_squared_Crop_Specific
Maize 124.17 0.57 135.70 0.58
Rice 202.56 0.59 213.72 0.58
Sorghum 187.00 0.82 241.98 0.71
B.Millet 173.11 0.93 193.00 0.91
F.Millet 367.62 0.70 523.83 0.56
Wheat 284.04 0.73 294.00 0.70
Beans 208.49 0.75 221.75 0.74
Potato 132.85 0.70 133.20 0.69 
write.csv(comparison_df_wide_ar, "Temporal-split-results_AR.csv", row.names = FALSE)

3. Random split validation

Here the dataset is split once (70:30). The model is trained on one part of the data (70%) and evaluated on the remaining set of the data (30%).

# Prepare Pooled Data to Split
mypts <- as.data.frame(mypts)
# sapply(mypts, class)
rows <- sample(x = 1:nrow(mypts), size = 0.70 * nrow(mypts))
train_pooled <- mypts[rows, ]
test_pooled  <- mypts[-rows, ]


# Hyperparameter Tuning for Pooled Model
# Define predictors for pooled (including crop dummies)
predictors_pooled <- c("maize", "rice", "sorghum", "bmillet", "fmillet", "wheat", "beans", "potato", "Month", "Year", "CPI", "ttport_1", "ttcity_u5", "popdens", 
"bio_3", "bio_6", "bio_9", "bio_12", "bio_18", "rain.sum.lag")

x_vars_p <- train_pooled[, predictors_pooled]
y_var_p  <- train_pooled$pkg_real

cat("Tuning Pooled Model...\n")
## Tuning Pooled Model...
trf_p <- tuneRF(x = x_vars_p, y = y_var_p, stepFactor = 1.5, improve = 0.01, ntreeTry = 500, plot = FALSE, trace = FALSE)
## -0.1762552 0.01 
## 0.06013946 0.01 
## 0.001910389 0.01
best_mtry_p <- trf_p[which.min(trf_p[, 2]), 1]

# Fit Final Pooled Model
RF_pooled <- randomForest(pkg_real ~ ., data = train_pooled[, c("pkg_real", predictors_pooled)], mtry = best_mtry_p, ntree = 1000, nodesize = 5, importance = TRUE)

RF_pooled
## 
## Call:
##  randomForest(formula = pkg_real ~ ., data = train_pooled[, c("pkg_real",      predictors_pooled)], mtry = best_mtry_p, ntree = 1000, nodesize = 5,      importance = TRUE) 
##                Type of random forest: regression
##                      Number of trees: 1000
## No. of variables tried at each split: 13
## 
##           Mean of squared residuals: 34921.41
##                     % Var explained: 92.85
# Evaluation Function: Pooled vs. Crop-Specific
evaluate_models <- function(target_crop) {
  cat("Evaluating Crop:", target_crop, "\n")
  
  # Filter data for this specific crop
  crop_data <- mypts[mypts$Crop == target_crop, ]
  
  # Split this crop's data 70/30
  set.seed(1983) 
  rows_c <- sample(x = 1:nrow(crop_data), size = 0.70 * nrow(crop_data))
  train_c <- crop_data[rows_c, ]
  test_c  <- crop_data[-rows_c, ]
  
  # POOLED MODEL PERFORMANCE 
  # Use the big RF_pooled to predict on this crop's specific test set
  pred_pooled <- predict(RF_pooled, newdata = test_c)
  
  # CROP-SPECIFIC MODEL
  # Predictors excluding crop dummies (since they are constant for one crop)
  predictors_c <- c("Month", "Year", "CPI", "ttport_1", "ttcity_u5", "popdens", 
                    "bio_3", "bio_6", "bio_9", "bio_12", "bio_18","rain.sum.lag")
  
  # Tune specifically for this crop to be fair
  trf_c <- tuneRF(x = train_c[, predictors_c], y = train_c$pkg_real, stepFactor = 1.5, improve = 0.01, ntreeTry = 500, plot = FALSE, trace = FALSE) 
  
  best_mtry_c <- trf_c[which.min(trf_c[, 2]), 1]
  rf_specific <- randomForest(pkg_real ~ ., data = train_c[, c("pkg_real", predictors_c)],mtry = best_mtry_c, ntree = 1000, nodesize = 5)
  
  pred_specific <- predict(rf_specific, newdata = test_c)
  
  # METRICS 
  metrics <- data.frame(
    Crop = target_crop,
    RMSE_Pooled = round(sqrt(mean((test_c$pkg_real - pred_pooled)^2, na.rm=TRUE)), 2),
    R2_Pooled   = round(summary(lm(test_c$pkg_real ~ pred_pooled))$r.squared, 2),
    RMSE_Specific = round(sqrt(mean((test_c$pkg_real - pred_specific)^2, na.rm=TRUE)), 2),
    R2_Specific   = round(summary(lm(test_c$pkg_real ~ pred_specific))$r.squared, 2)
  )
  
  return(metrics)
}
# Run and save results
all_crops <- unique(mypts$Crop)
comparison_results_tt <- do.call(rbind, lapply(all_crops, evaluate_models))
## Evaluating Crop: 1 
## -0.0936124 0.01 
## 0.04856359 0.01 
## 0.005127983 0.01 
## Evaluating Crop: 2 
## -0.1029928 0.01 
## 0.04394681 0.01 
## -0.03688437 0.01 
## Evaluating Crop: 3 
## -0.02377015 0.01 
## 0.04034933 0.01 
## -0.01640377 0.01 
## Evaluating Crop: 4 
## -0.03582559 0.01 
## -0.02799871 0.01 
## Evaluating Crop: 5 
## -0.03113707 0.01 
## -0.006846908 0.01 
## Evaluating Crop: 6 
## -0.05838032 0.01 
## 0.002921601 0.01 
## Evaluating Crop: 7 
## -0.08313875 0.01 
## 0.02743352 0.01 
## 0.003001993 0.01 
## Evaluating Crop: 8 
## -0.02621305 0.01 
## 0.01818056 0.01 
## -0.001453224 0.01
print(comparison_results_tt)
##       Crop RMSE_Pooled R2_Pooled RMSE_Specific R2_Specific
## 1    Maize       96.13      0.82        117.73        0.74
## 2     Rice      125.02      0.93        178.19        0.85
## 3  Sorghum      144.32      0.88        221.49        0.71
## 4 B.Millet      106.80      0.96        206.40        0.83
## 5 F.Millet      161.36      0.88        225.09        0.75
## 6    Wheat      179.96      0.88        282.50        0.68
## 7    Beans      143.24      0.90        201.28        0.80
## 8   Potato       92.41      0.81        116.04        0.70
comparison_df_wide3 <- comparison_results_tt %>%
  rename(
    RMSE_Pooled = RMSE_Pooled,
    R_squared_Pooled = R2_Pooled,
    RMSE_Crop_Specific = RMSE_Specific,
    R_squared_Crop_Specific = R2_Specific)
knitr::kable(comparison_df_wide3, digits = 2, caption = "Table 3: Model Comparison (70/30 Train-Test Split Validation)")
Table 3: Model Comparison (70/30 Train-Test Split Validation)
Crop RMSE_Pooled R_squared_Pooled RMSE_Crop_Specific R_squared_Crop_Specific

Rice 125.02 0.93 178.19 0.85
Sorghum 144.32 0.88 221.49 0.71
B.Millet 106.80 0.96 206.40 0.83
F.Millet 161.36 0.88 225.09 0.75
Wheat 179.96 0.88 282.50 0.68
Beans 143.24 0.90 201.28 0.80
Potato 92.41 0.81 116.04 0.70 
write.csv(comparison_df_wide3, "Train-Test-Validation-results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Comparison of regional and predicted rural maize prices for January 2025

# Comparison of regional and predicted rural maize prices
# We use the deflated prices (pkg_real) for 2025
mypts_df <- as.data.frame(mypts) %>% 
  filter(Year == 2025, Crop == "Maize") %>%
  dplyr::select(Region_GADM, Month, pkg_real) 
# The comparison loop
comparison_list <- list()

for (m in 1:12) {
  message("Processing month: ", m)
  
  # Load Prediction Raster (trained on deflated prices)
  pred_file <- sprintf("C:/Users/LMADAGA/OneDrive - CGIAR/H/Tanzania Price data/Datasets/Pred-plots/Maize/maize_price_rf_pred_%02d.tif", m)
  if (!file.exists(pred_file)) next
  
  pred_maize <- rast(pred_file) |> project(crs(tza1))
  names(pred_maize) <- "Predicted"
  
  # Calculate Regional Benchmark using pkg_real
  dta_reg <- mypts_df %>%
    filter(Month == m) %>%
    group_by(Region_GADM) %>%
    summarise(Reg_max_pkg = max(pkg_real, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
    ungroup()
  
  # Apply Njombe patch
  if("Iringa" %in% dta_reg$Region_GADM & !"Njombe" %in% dta_reg$Region_GADM){
    iringa_val <- dta_reg$Reg_max_pkg[dta_reg$Region_GADM == "Iringa"]
    dta_reg <- bind_rows(dta_reg, tibble(Region_GADM = "Njombe", Reg_max_pkg = iringa_val))
  }
  
  # Spatial Join to Raster
  tza1_with_reg <- merge(tza1, dta_reg, by.x = "NAME_1", by.y = "Region_GADM", all.x = TRUE)
  regional_raster <- rasterize(tza1_with_reg, pred_maize, field = "Reg_max_pkg")
  
  # Data Extraction
  # Convert raster to dataframe and extract regional values
  tmp_df <- as.data.frame(pred_maize, xy = TRUE, na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Get regional max values for each pixel coordinate
  reg_vals <- terra::extract(regional_raster, tmp_df[,c("x","y")])
  tmp_df$Regional_Max <- reg_vals[,2] # Extract the second column (the values)
  
  # Calculate Difference
  tmp_df <- tmp_df %>%
    mutate(
      Diff = Regional_Max - Predicted, 
      Month = m
    )
  
  # Attach Region Names for the X-axis
  pts_tmp <- vect(tmp_df, geom = c("x", "y"), crs = crs(tza1))
  tmp_df$Region <- terra::extract(tza1["NAME_1"], pts_tmp)[,2]
  
  comparison_list[[m]] <- tmp_df
}
## Processing month: 1
## Processing month: 2
## Processing month: 3
## Processing month: 4
## Processing month: 5
## Processing month: 6
## Processing month: 7
## Processing month: 8
## Processing month: 9
## Processing month: 10
## Processing month: 11
## Processing month: 12
comparison_all <- bind_rows(comparison_list) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Region), !is.na(Diff))

comparison_all$Month_Label <- factor(comparison_all$Month, levels = 1:12, labels = month.name)
# Filter specifically for January
january_comparison <- comparison_all %>%
  filter(Month == 1) 

mrp <- ggplot(january_comparison, aes(x = Region, y = Diff)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "#A6CEE3", color = "black", outlier.size = 0.3, alpha = 0.7, size = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.6) +
  # Note: facet_wrap is removed to focus on the single month
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Real Regional Price - Real Predicted Price (TZS/kg)",
    title = "",
    subtitle = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1, size = 9), # Tilted for better readability
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, linewidth = 0.5))

mrp

Prediction Evaluation

Using 2025/2026 Validation data

pred<-predict(object=rf, newdata=validation_data)
actual<-validation_data$pkg_real
result<-data.frame(actual=actual, predicted=pred)
mse <- mean((actual - pred)^2, na.rm=TRUE)
paste('Mean Squared Error:', mse)
## [1] "Mean Squared Error: 148714.881388047"
rmse <- sqrt(mse)
paste('Root Mean Squared error: ',mean(sqrt(rf$mse)))
## [1] "Root Mean Squared error:  167.261827665718"
#Save predicted & observed price
write.csv(result, "result.csv")
#reading result.csv file (predicted vs observed)
rslt <- read.csv("result.csv", header=T)
print(names(rslt))
## [1] "X"         "actual"    "predicted"
#RMSE predicting from rf - predicited vs observed 
rf.rmse<-round(sqrt(mean( (rslt$actual-rslt$predicted)^2 , na.rm = TRUE )),2)
print(rf.rmse)
## [1] 385.64
#R-square
rf.r2<-round(summary(lm(actual~predicted, rslt))$r.squared,2)
print(rf.r2)
## [1] 0.73
range(actual)
## [1]  290.3126 3545.8025
range(pred)
## [1]  389.4224 3325.9422
#plotting predicted Vs observed
ggplot(result, aes(x=actual, y=predicted), alpha=0.6) +
  geom_point(colour = "blue", size = 1.4, alpha=0.6) +
  ggtitle('Random Forest "Wholesale Grain Prices in Tanzania"') +
  scale_x_continuous("Observed Price (Tsh) Per Kg",
                     limits = c(0, 5000),
                     breaks = seq(0, 5000, 1000)) +
  scale_y_continuous("Predicted Price (Tsh) Per Kg",
                     limits = c(0, 5000),
                     breaks = seq(0, 5000, 1000)) +
  theme(axis.line = element_line(colour = "black"),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8, angle = 90, hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8)) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(aes(x = actual, y = predicted), formula = y ~ x, method = "lm", se = FALSE, colour = "red", linetype = 2, size = 0.9) +
  annotate("text", x = 300, y = 4500, label = paste("RMSE:", rf.rmse)) +
  annotate("text", x = 300, y = 4200, label = paste("R^2: ", rf.r2), parse = TRUE)
## Warning in fortify(data, ...): Arguments in `...` must be used.
## ✖ Problematic argument:
## • alpha = 0.6
## ℹ Did you misspell an argument name?
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

Partial dependence plots

Plot Partial dependence of all the variables used except food commodities and months.

library(caret)

var_importance <- varImp(rf)

impvar <- rownames(var_importance)[order(var_importance[, 1], decreasing=TRUE)]
op <- par(mfrow=c(2, 4))
# exclude crop commodities and months
predictors_to_plot <- setdiff(impvar, c("maize", "rice", "sorghum", "bmillet", "fmillet", "wheat", "beans", "potato", "jan", "feb", "mar", "apr", "may", "jun", "jul", "aug", "sep", "oct", "nov", "dec"))

for (i in seq_along(predictors_to_plot)) {
  partialPlot(rf, as.data.frame(training_data), predictors_to_plot[i], xlab=predictors_to_plot[i],
              main="Partial Dependence")
}

Some descriptive statistics for each crop

# Compute descriptive statistics for each crop
stats <- prices.monthly.long %>%
  filter(pkg_real > 0) %>%  
  group_by(Crop) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(pkg_real, na.rm = TRUE),
    Median = median(pkg_real, na.rm = TRUE),
    Minimum = min(pkg_real, na.rm = TRUE),
    Maximum = max(pkg_real, na.rm = TRUE),
    Std_Dev = sd(pkg_real, na.rm = TRUE),
    IQR = IQR(pkg_real, na.rm = TRUE),
    Observations = n()
  ) %>%
  arrange(Crop)

print(stats)
## # A tibble: 8 × 8
##   Crop      Mean Median Minimum Maximum Std_Dev   IQR Observations
##   <fct>    <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>        <int>
## 1 Maize     725.   691.    275.   1739.    227.  340.         1398
## 2 Rice     2078.  2080.    749.   3465.    450.  608.         1414
## 3 Sorghum  1226.  1206.    290.   2969.    427.  612.         1209
## 4 B.Millet 1332.  1189.    381.   3608.    556.  751.         1017
## 5 F.Millet 1714.  1695.    624.   3261.    471.  487.         1246
## 6 Wheat    1679.  1660.    341.   3594.    499.  564.          973
## 7 Beans    2301.  2306.    950    3621.    469.  730.         1405
## 8 Potato    818.   802.    300.   1613.    218.  228.         1365
